title: Scope-normalization helper duplicated in 5 locations with divergent signatures; canonical normalizeScopeContext under-adopted
evidence: Five implementations of the same trim+empty-to-null logic: normalizeScopeValue (unknown) in reconsolidation-bridge + lineage-state; normalizeScopeMatchValue (string|null) in types.ts; inline closure in preflight.ts; canonical normalizeScopeContext in scope-governance.ts. Four callers reinvented local versions instead of importing the canonical helper. Iter 2 confirmed two of these are semantically identical — confirming the drift risk is real.
impact: Latent R1-P1-001-class drift: any future modification of one copy without finding the others silently re-introduces empty-string-scope CAS bypass.
recommendation: Collapse 4 reinvented versions to imports of normalizeScopeContext; add unit test enforcing single source of truth.

claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether divergent signatures are intentional — they are not; types.ts and reconsolidation-bridge agree semantically. Checked whether the canonical normalizeScopeContext handles unknown input — it does via normalizeId.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Local-file helpers to avoid import cycles; but dedup.ts already imports normalizeScopeContext transitively via memory-search.ts, so no cycle blocks adoption.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Collapse to imports + unit test OR README explaining intentional divergence

title: description.json.lastUpdated has no auto-refresh mechanism; R3-P2-001 drift is structurally inevitable
evidence: generate-context.js refreshes graph-metadata.json per its comments but contains zero references to lastUpdated across all 571 lines. Same for other 8 dist/memory/ scripts. No .git/hooks are active (all .sample). No .husky or .githooks directory. The drift R3-P2-001 observed is the predictable outcome of zero infrastructure, not a one-off lapse.
impact: Memory-search consumers reading description.json as source-of-truth for folder status get stale snapshots whenever /memory:save cadence falls behind commit cadence. Phase 016 targeted canonical-packet hygiene, making this a scope gap.
recommendation: Add lastUpdated writer to generate-context.js during canonical save; OR add .git/hooks/post-commit to auto-refresh; OR amend CLAUDE.md §3 to declare description.json.lastUpdated is manually maintained.

claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked for lastUpdated-writing helper under a different name in generate-context.js — zero matches. Checked CLAUDE.md §3 wording — says 'description.json and graph-metadata.json are auto-generated/refreshed by generate-context.js during canonical saves' which is only accurate for graph-metadata.json.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Team may consider it manual-edit; but description.json is machine-parsed and cited as authoritative, contradicting manual-maintenance intent.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: generate-context.js updated to write lastUpdated OR post-commit hook added OR CLAUDE.md amended

title: runPostInsertEnrichment() is a 243-line god function with 5 near-identical try/catch enrichment blocks
evidence: Function handles 5 enrichment domains (causalLinks, entityExtraction, summaries, entityLinking, graphLifecycle) each with the same if-enabled / try-run / catch-exception / else-skip shape. Five copies of ~30-50 line blocks.
impact: Adding a 6th domain is a high-friction change; future bug fix in one copy may not propagate to others.
recommendation: Extract runEnrichmentStep(name, isEnabled, runner, options) helper with per-domain hooks for logging/warnings. Reduces function to ~80 lines.

title: No compile-time exhaustiveness checks (satisfies never / assertNever) anywhere in mcp_server/
file: .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/ (grep: 0 hits for satisfies never | exhaustiveCheck | assertNever)
evidence: Seven+ typed unions benefit from exhaustiveness checks: OnIndexSkipReason (4), EnrichmentStepStatus (5), EnrichmentSkipReason (10), EnrichmentFailureReason (7), ConflictAbortStatus (2), HookStateLoadFailureReason (7), SharedPayloadTrustState. Consumers use Record<string,...> object-lookup + string compare instead of switch with assertNever. Concrete impact at post-insert.ts:302-306: reasonMap has 3 entries for 4-member OnIndexSkipReason; 4th is manually special-cased; a 5th would silently fall through to a generic fallback.
impact: Silent drift: adding a new union member never errors at compile time; runtime falls through to generic fallback with semantically wrong behavior.
recommendation: Add const _exhaustive: never = value; assertion to consumers of typed unions. Candidates: post-insert.ts graphLifecycle branch, shared-payload.ts trust-state producers.

title: executeConflict() has two near-duplicate CAS transaction blocks sharing identical precondition checks
evidence: Both transaction blocks perform the same 3 precondition checks in the same order: currentRow + archived check, hasScopeRetagged, hasPredecessorChanged. Then diverge: one UPDATE-to-deprecated+insertSupersedesEdge, the other UPDATE-content+optional-embedding. Both end with same changes===0 guard.
impact: One branch fixing a predecessor-check bug while the other stays broken is a realistic drift scenario.
recommendation: Extract runAtomicReconsolidationTxn(predecessorSnapshot, op, ops) with shared preamble and branch-specific body.

title: importance_tier != 'deprecated' is the de facto deprecation predicate, repeated inline in 6+ SQL statements without shared symbol
evidence: Deprecation contract encoded as string literal 'deprecated' compared against importance_tier inline in multiple SQL statements. No exported DEPRECATED_TIER constant or isLiveRow() helper. isArchivedRow() helper exists at line 494 (archived status) but no equivalent for deprecation.
impact: If deprecation vocabulary extends (e.g. 'superseded', 'tombstoned'), every inline comparison must be hunted individually.
recommendation: Extract LIVE_ROW_CLAUSE SQL fragment constant or isLiveTier(tier) predicate.

recommendedNextFocus: Iteration 5: CROSS-REFERENCE / SYNTHESIS — consolidate root-cause clusters (scope-normalization drift, canonical-save-surface hygiene, ascii-only sanitization theme). Revisit R1-P1-001 iter 2 downgrade; assess P1->P0 upgrades; decide whether R4-P2-002 (exhaustiveness gap) should upgrade to P1.
